Create error.tsx files at each route segment level to provide granular error boundaries
Always include a global-error.tsx at the app root to catch errors in the root layout
Use not-found.tsx files to handle 404 errors with user-friendly pages at appropriate route levels
Call the notFound() function from next/navigation when data lookups return null/undefined in Server Components
Implement try/catch blocks in Server Actions and return structured error responses instead of throwing
Use the 'error' prop in error.tsx components to display meaningful messages and provide a 'reset' retry button
Log errors server-side with structured logging (request ID, user context, stack trace) before returning error responses
Validate all user inputs in Server Actions and API Route Handlers using Zod or similar schemas before processing
Return appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 500) from Route Handlers using NextResponse
Use loading.tsx and Suspense boundaries to handle async states gracefully and prevent layout shift
Implement middleware for cross-cutting concerns like authentication checks and redirect unauthorized users with proper status codes
Add error monitoring and reporting (Sentry, Datadog) with Next.js instrumentation.ts for server-side observability
Handle redirect() calls from next/navigation outside of try/catch blocks since redirect throws a special error internally
